summary.intent: Review PR changes to verify all SQL column names are correct across modified queries.
summary.outcome: Found and fixed incorrect column names in the RECOMPUTE_BOOKED_FROM_PROC query in allocation_dao.rs — `int_cores` and `int_gpus` corrected to `int_cores_reserved` and `int_gpus_reserved` on the proc table.

repo: OpenCue Rust scheduler SQL queries live in rust/crates/scheduler/src/dao/

code.finding: RECOMPUTE_BOOKED_FROM_PROC query had wrong column names: p.int_cores and p.int_gpus should be p.int_cores_reserved and p.int_gpus_reserved. FLUSH_SUBSCRIPTION_DELTA query was correct.

workflow: When reviewing PR diffs with SQL queries, cross-reference column names against actual DB schema rather than assuming consistency across tables.

friction: Column naming is inconsistent between tables (proc uses _reserved suffix, subscription does not), which makes it easy to write incorrect queries by analogy.
